name: Rogig (Grape Walnut Candy)
recipeInstructions:
1. String the walnut halves on 24-inch long cotton thread, leaving extra string at both ends.
2. Mix flour, sugar, cornstarch, and corn syrup in a bowl until smooth.
3. Add grape juice to the mixture and stir until combined.
4. Pour half of the mixture into a heavy saucepan and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Boil until thick. Remove from heat.
5. Dip walnut strings one at a time into the hot grape juice mixture several times, allowing excess coating to drip back into the pan.
6. Place walnuts on a drying rack (a broom handle propped between two chairs works well). Dry overnight.
7. Reserve leftover cooked grape mixture for later use.
8. The next day, pass remaining mixture through a sieve and add it to the saucepan with the dried grapes.
9. Dip walnut strings several more times in the hot grape mixture.
10. Hang walnuts to dry for several days in a dark, cool place.
11. To serve, remove walnuts from the string and roll them in powdered sugar. Store in a covered jar or tin.
NOTE: White grape juice or other fruit juices such as pear or apple can be used.
